text:
CT SCAN - CHEST, ABDOMEN, AND PELVIS WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST
POST-ALLOGENEIC STEM CELL TRANSPLANT RESTAGING EXAMINATION (6-MONTH)

CLINICAL INDICATION:
48-year-old female with a history of AML with myelodysplasia-related changes (AML-MRC), FLT3-ITD high allelic ratio, ELN adverse risk. Treated with 7+3 induction chemotherapy (cytarabine/daunorubicin) plus midostaurin, achieved morphologic and MRD-negative complete remission after one induction cycle, followed by one cycle of high-dose cytarabine (HiDAC) consolidation. Subsequently underwent 10/10 matched unrelated donor (MUD) allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplant with reduced-intensity conditioning (fludarabine/busulfan). Course post-transplant complicated by CMV reactivation (resolved with valganciclovir) and mild chronic graft-versus-host disease (skin, oral, and ocular involvement; managed with topical therapies). Now presenting for 6-month post-transplant restaging CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis to evaluate for AML relapse, extramedullary disease, and transplant-related pulmonary or abdominal complications.

TECHNIQUE:
Multidetector CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis was performed following the administration of 100 mL of intravenous iohexol contrast agent (Omnipaque 350), with images acquired in the portal venous phase. Coronal and sagittal reformatted images were reviewed in addition to axial images. Lung windows, soft tissue windows, and bone windows were utilized. No oral contrast was administered per protocol. No adverse reaction to intravenous contrast was noted.

Comparison: CT chest, abdomen, and pelvis with contrast from pre-induction staging workup (spleen 14.2 cm at that time); PET-CT performed at pre-transplant restaging (spleen 12.1 cm at that time).

---

FINDINGS:

CHEST:

Lungs and Airways:
The lungs are fully expanded bilaterally. The central airways, including the trachea, carina, and visualized mainstem bronchi, are patent and of normal caliber. There are no endobronchial lesions or airway narrowing. The lung parenchyma appears clear and well-aerated throughout. No focal consolidation, lobar or segmental atelectasis, or mass lesion is identified in either lung. No ground-glass opacities are seen, and there is no evidence of an organizing pneumonia or bronchiolitis obliterans pattern. No pulmonary nodules are identified above the threshold of detection. No cavitary lesions and no tree-in-bud nodularity to suggest active infectious or inflammatory bronchiolar disease. The interstitial architecture appears preserved without reticulation, honeycombing, or traction bronchiectasis.

Pleura:
There are no pleural effusions, right or left. No pleural thickening or pleural-based masses. No pneumothorax.

Mediastinum and Hila:
The mediastinum is midline in position. The thymus demonstrates expected fatty involution for patient age. No anterior, middle, or posterior mediastinal masses. The mediastinal and hilar lymph nodes are within normal size limits; no individual node measures greater than 1.0 cm in short-axis diameter, and none demonstrate pathologic morphology (rounded contour, loss of fatty hilum). Specifically, the subcarinal, right and left paratracheal, aortopulmonary window, precarinal, and bilateral hilar nodal stations are all unremarkable. No conglomerate adenopathy.

Heart and Pericardium:
The cardiac silhouette is normal in size. No pericardial effusion. The visualized coronary arteries show no gross calcification. Aortic root and ascending thoracic aorta are of normal caliber.

Chest Wall and Bony Thorax:
No lytic or blastic lesions are identified in the ribs, sternum, clavicles, or visualized proximal humeri. No pathologic fractures. Soft tissues of the chest wall are unremarkable.

---

ABDOMEN:

Liver:
The liver is normal in size with a smooth contour. The hepatic parenchyma demonstrates homogeneous attenuation without focal hypo- or hyperattenuating lesions. The intrahepatic bile ducts are not dilated. The portal vein is patent and normal in caliber. The hepatic veins drain normally into the inferior vena cava without evidence of occlusion or narrowing that would suggest sinusoidal obstruction syndrome (hepatic veno-occlusive disease). The gallbladder is present and appears unremarkable without wall thickening or cholelithiasis.

Spleen:
The spleen measures approximately 9.8 cm in craniocaudal dimension, within normal limits and representing substantial interval decrease compared with the 14.2 cm craniocaudal dimension documented at the time of AML diagnosis and the 12.1 cm seen on pre-transplant PET-CT. The spleen demonstrates homogeneous enhancement without focal lesions, infarct, or masses. No perisplenic fluid. The previously documented splenomegaly has fully resolved.

Pancreas:
The pancreas is normal in size, shape, and attenuation throughout the head, neck, body, and tail. The pancreatic duct is not dilated. No peripancreatic fat stranding or fluid collections.

Kidneys and Ureters:
The kidneys are symmetric and normal in size bilaterally, with smooth contours and uniform cortical enhancement. No hydronephrosis. No urolithiasis. No renal masses or cysts of significance. The renal collecting systems and proximal ureters appear unobstructed.

Adrenal Glands:
Bilateral adrenal glands are normal in size and morphology. No adrenal masses or nodular thickening.

Bowel:
The stomach, small bowel, and large bowel are normal in caliber. No wall thickening, mucosal irregularity, or focal lesion to suggest bowel-wall involvement by GVHD or other pathology. No evidence of bowel obstruction. No free intraperitoneal air.

Abdominal Vasculature:
The abdominal aorta is normal in caliber. The celiac axis, superior and inferior mesenteric arteries are patent at their origins. The inferior vena cava is normal in caliber and demonstrates appropriate opacification without filling defect to suggest thrombosis.

Abdominal and Retroperitoneal Lymph Nodes:
No enlarged lymph nodes are identified in the periportal, peripancreatic, gastrohepatic ligament, para-aortic, aortocaval, paracaval, or mesenteric regions. No retroperitoneal soft tissue masses.

Peritoneum and Ascites:
No free peritoneal fluid. No peritoneal nodularity or omental thickening.

---

PELVIS:

Bladder:
The urinary bladder is partially filled and appears unremarkable without wall thickening or intraluminal lesion.

Gynecologic Structures:
The uterus is normal in size and morphology. No adnexal masses or free pelvic fluid.

Pelvic Lymph Nodes:
No enlarged external iliac, internal iliac, obturator, or inguinal lymph nodes identified.

Bony Pelvis:
The visualized osseous structures of the pelvis, including the iliac wings, acetabula, and proximal femora bilaterally, demonstrate no lytic or sclerotic lesions. The bone density and trabecular pattern appear appropriate. No evidence of avascular necrosis of the femoral heads, which can be a late complication of corticosteroid use and conditioning chemotherapy, is identified on this examination.

Soft Tissues:
No soft tissue masses or fluid collections in the pelvis. No evidence of extramedullary hematopoiesis or chloromatous deposits.

---

BONE WINDOWS (AXIAL SKELETON):
The visualized spine demonstrates normal vertebral body heights and alignment without compression fractures. The pedicles and posterior elements appear intact. Bone density appears grossly preserved without diffuse demineralization. No focal lytic or blastic lesions are identified throughout the thoracic or lumbar spine, or in the sacrum. No paraspinal soft tissue masses. No evidence of periosteal reaction.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. No evidence of AML relapse, chloromatous deposits, or extramedullary leukemic disease. The chest, abdomen, and pelvis are clear of hyperattenuating or mass-forming lesions that would raise concern for myeloid sarcoma.

2. The splenomegaly that was present at the time of AML diagnosis (14.2 cm) and at pre-transplant evaluation (12.1 cm) has fully resolved; the spleen now measures within normal limits at approximately 9.8 cm. This finding is consistent with successful treatment response and disease remission.

3. No lymphadenopathy identified in any nodal station of the chest, abdomen, or pelvis.

4. The lungs are clear without consolidation, ground-glass opacities, nodules, or features to suggest bronchiolitis obliterans, organizing pneumonia, or active opportunistic infection. No pleural effusions. This is reassuring in the context of mild chronic GVHD and prior treatment with high-dose cytarabine.

5. No hepatomegaly. Hepatic parenchyma appears homogeneous without focal lesions. The hepatic venous anatomy is unremarkable without features of sinusoidal obstruction syndrome.

6. No avascular necrosis of the femoral heads identified on current examination.

7. No bony lesions throughout the imaged skeleton to suggest extramedullary or intraosseous leukemic involvement.

Overall, this CT examination is entirely consistent with ongoing complete remission at 6 months following matched unrelated donor allogeneic stem cell transplant, with no imaging evidence of disease recurrence or significant transplant-related complications.

REPORTING RADIOLOGIST: [Attending Radiologist, Body Imaging Division]
STATUS: Final
